translation_text: He who has in himself abundantly the attributes (of the Dao) is like an infant.
translation_text_raw: He who has in himself abundantly the attributes (of the Dao) is like an infant.

translation_text: Poisonous insects will not sting him; fierce beasts will not seize him;
translation_text_raw: Poisonous insects will not sting him; fierce beasts will not seize him;

translation_text: birds of prey will not strike him.
translation_text_raw: birds of prey will not strike him.

chinese_text: 攫鳥不搏。骨弱筋柔而握固。
translation_text: (The infant's) bones are weak and its sinews soft, but yet its grasp is firm.
translation_text_raw: (The infant's) bones are weak and its sinews soft, but yet its grasp is firm.

translation_text: It knows not yet the union of male and female, and yet its virile member may be excited;
translation_text_raw: It knows not yet the union of male and female, and yet its virile member may be excited;

translation_text: - showing the perfection of its physical essence.
translation_text_raw: - showing the perfection of its physical essence.

translation_text: All day long it will cry without its throat becoming hoarse; - showing the harmony (in its constitution).
translation_text_raw: All day long it will cry without its throat becoming hoarse; - showing the harmony (in its constitution).

translation_text: To him by whom this harmony is known,
translation_text_raw: To him by whom this harmony is known,

translation_text: (The secret of) the unchanging (Dao) is shown,
translation_text_raw: (The secret of) the unchanging (Dao) is shown,

translation_text: And in the knowledge wisdom finds its throne.
translation_text_raw: And in the knowledge wisdom finds its throne.

translation_text: All life-increasing arts to evil turn;
translation_text_raw: All life-increasing arts to evil turn;

translation_text: Where the mind makes the vital breath to burn,
translation_text_raw: Where the mind makes the vital breath to burn,

translation_text: When things have become strong, they (then) become old, which may be said to be contrary to the Dao.
translation_text_raw: When things have become strong, they (then) become old, which may be said to be contrary to the Dao.

translation_text: Whatever is contrary to the Dao soon ends.
translation_text_raw: Whatever is contrary to the Dao soon ends.
